files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index c462284..8384e3d 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -247,2 +247,8 @@ Day 238 / Week 34 observations:
 - Started tier-10 R&D. It rolled an unusually fast 30-day expected completion and large +1.7232 expected quality boost, making it worth the cash drawdown. Also started level-2 research for E1/E2/E3 to improve enterprise pricing accuracy.
 - Week 34 adjustment: keep tier-6 capacity and product settings stable, reduce global ops to $50k/day and S2 targeted ops to $75k/day to protect cash after tier-10, keep S2 dev and maintenance content spend, and post a roadmap/reliability update.
+
+Day 245 / Week 35 observations:
+- Cash is $1.72M after tier-10 funding. Individual subs rose to 106,987, but cancellations increased to 749 and open issues rose to 3,839 after support was trimmed.
+- Level-2 enterprise research completed. Estimated quality floors are much higher than expected for E1/E2: E1 q_min about 2.29, E2 about 2.54, E3 about 1.78. Current enterprise C quality is only 1.84, so E1/E2 need either targeted quality work or tier-10 completion before broad conversion.
+- 131 new enterprise leads arrived with 66,660 seats. Sent the same low-friction pricing: E1 $7/$12/$20, E2 $9/$17/$28, E3 $12/$25/$42.
+- Week 35 adjustment: restore some support to global ops $60k/day and S2 ops $90k/day, and start a temporary targeted enterprise dev push: E1 $40k/day, E2 $80k/day, E3 $30k/day, while keeping S2 dev $18k/day and maintenance S2 content spend. Tier-10 is expected day 268 and should close the remaining quality gap.
